title: End-to-End: From Evidence to Submission Decision
code: library(r4sub) #> -- Attaching R4SUB packages (r4sub 0.1.0) ------------------------------ #>   r4subcore    0.1.0 #>   r4subtrace   0.1.0 #>   r4subscore   0.1.0 #>   r4subrisk    0.1.0 #>   r4subdata    0.1.1 #>   r4subprofile 0.1.0 library(r4subdata)

title: End-to-End: From Evidence to Submission Decision
text: evidence_pharma contains 250 evidence rows study CDISCPILOT01 across four readiness pillars.

title: End-to-End: From Evidence to Submission Decision
text: Pillar scores contributing SCI
code: pillar_scores <- compute_pillar_scores(evidence_pharma) pillar_scores[, c("pillar", "pillar_score", "n_indicators", "weight")] #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   pillar    pillar_score n_indicators weight #>   <chr>            <dbl>        <int>  <dbl> #> 1 quality          0.396            8   0.35 #> 2 trace            0.439            4   0.25 #> 3 risk             0.499            3   0.25 #> 4 usability        0.472            3   0.15  sci <- compute_sci(pillar_scores) cat("SCI:", sci$SCI, "\n") #> SCI: 44.4 cat("Band:", sci$band, "\n") #> Band: high_risk scores_vec <- setNames(pillar_scores$pillar_score, pillar_scores$pillar) cols <- c(quality = "#2C6DB5", trace = "#27AE60",           risk = "#E74C3C", usability = "#F39C12")  par(mar = c(4, 6, 3, 2)) barplot(   scores_vec[names(cols)],   horiz  = TRUE,   las    = 1,   col    = cols[names(scores_vec)],   border = NA,   xlim   = c(0, 1),   xlab   = "Score (0-1)",   main   = paste0("Pillar Scores  |  SCI = ", round(sci$SCI, 1),                   "  [", sci$band, "]") ) abline(v = 0.85, lty = 2, col = "#555555")

what: 4. Understand what is driving the score
title: End-to-End: From Evidence to Submission Decision
text: Top 8 indicators SCI loss contribution
code: expl     <- sci_explain(evidence_pharma) top_loss <- head(expl$indicator_contributions, 8) top_loss[, c("indicator_id", "indicator_name", "indicator_score", "loss")] #> # A tibble: 8 × 4 #>   indicator_id     indicator_name     indicator_score  loss #>   <chr>            <chr>                        <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 R-HIGH-RPN       High RPN Item                0.323  5.64 #> 2 T-ORPHAN-VAR     Orphan Variable              0.292  4.43 #> 3 T-TRACE-LEVEL    Traceability Level           0.362  3.98 #> 4 R-MITIGATION-GAP Mitigation Gap               0.527  3.94 #> 5 Q-TYPE-MISMATCH  Type Mismatch                0.259  3.24 #> 6 Q-FORMAT-ERR     Format Error                 0.321  2.97 #> 7 R-OPEN-RISK      Open Risk                    0.648  2.94 #> 8 T-AMBIGUOUS-MAP  Ambiguous Mapping            0.533  2.92 par(mar = c(4, 14, 3, 2)) barplot(   rev(top_loss$loss),   names.arg = rev(top_loss$indicator_name),   horiz  = TRUE,   las    = 1,   col    = "#E74C3C",   border = NA,   xlab   = "SCI loss",   main   = "Top Loss Contributors" )

title: End-to-End: From Evidence to Submission Decision
code:
prof <- submission_profile("FDA", "NDA") prof$pillar_weights #>   quality     trace      risk usability  #>      0.35      0.25      0.25      0.15  val <- validate_against_profile(evidence_pharma, prof) cat("Compliant:", val$is_compliant, "
") #> Compliant: FALSE cat("Coverage: ", round(val$coverage * 100, 1), "%
", sep = "") #> Coverage: 100% cfg_fda    <- profile_sci_config(prof) ps_fda     <- compute_pillar_scores(evidence_pharma, config = cfg_fda) sci_fda    <- compute_sci(ps_fda, config = cfg_fda) cat("FDA-calibrated SCI:", sci_fda$SCI, "[", sci_fda$band, "]
") #> FDA-calibrated SCI: 44.4 [ high_risk ]

title: End-to-End: From Evidence to Submission Decision
text: SCI different regulatory authority profiles
code: profiles <- list(   "FDA / NDA"    = submission_profile("FDA",  "NDA"),   "EMA / MAA"    = submission_profile("EMA",  "MAA"),   "PMDA / NDA"   = submission_profile("PMDA", "NDA_JP") )  sci_vals <- vapply(profiles, function(p) {   cfg <- profile_sci_config(p)   compute_sci(compute_pillar_scores(evidence_pharma, config = cfg),               config = cfg)$SCI }, numeric(1))  par(mar = c(4, 9, 3, 2)) barplot(   sci_vals,   horiz  = TRUE,   las    = 1,   col    = "#2C6DB5",   border = NA,   xlim   = c(0, 100),   xlab   = "SCI (0-100)",   main   = "SCI by Regulatory Authority Profile" ) abline(v = 85, lty = 2, col = "#27AE60") abline(v = 70, lty = 2, col = "#F39C12")

title: End-to-End: From Evidence to Submission Decision
text: Risk distribution severity level
code:
data(risk_register_pharma) rr           <- create_risk_register(risk_register_pharma) risk_scores  <- compute_risk_scores(rr)  cat("Mean RPN:        ", risk_scores$mean_rpn, "
") #> Mean RPN:         24.8 cat("Max RPN:         ", risk_scores$max_rpn, "
") #> Max RPN:          48 cat("Total risks:     ", risk_scores$n_risks, "
") #> Total risks:      18  print(risk_scores$risk_distribution) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   risk_level     n #>   <chr>      <int> #> 1 high           3 #> 2 low            4 #> 3 medium        11 rd        <- risk_scores$risk_distribution risk_cols <- c(low = "#27AE60", medium = "#F39C12",                high = "#E67E22", critical = "#E74C3C") rd_named  <- setNames(rd$n, rd$risk_level) rd_plot   <- rd_named[intersect(names(risk_cols), names(rd_named))]  par(mar = c(4, 5, 3, 2)) barplot(   rd_plot,   col    = risk_cols[names(rd_plot)],   border = NA,   ylab   = "Count",   main   = paste0("Risk Distribution  |  Mean RPN = ", risk_scores$mean_rpn),   las    = 1 )

title: End-to-End: From Evidence to Submission Decision
code: data(adam_metadata) data(sdtm_metadata) data(trace_mapping)  ctx      <- r4sub_run_context(study_id = "CDISCPILOT01", environment = "DEV") #> ℹ Run context created: "R4S-20260317053406-wl4dieex" tm       <- build_trace_model(adam_metadata, sdtm_metadata, trace_mapping) ev_trace <- trace_model_to_evidence(tm, ctx = ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 47 rows  ind_trace <- trace_indicator_scores(ev_trace) ind_trace #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   indicator                      value description                               #>   <chr>                          <dbl> <chr>                                     #> 1 TRACE_VAR_COVERAGE_L2PLUS      0.694 Proportion of ADaM variables with trace … #> 2 TRACE_VAR_COVERAGE_L3PLUS      0.694 Proportion of ADaM variables with trace … #> 3 TRACE_ORPHAN_VAR_COUNT        11     Number of orphan ADaM variables with no … #> 4 TRACE_AMBIGUOUS_MAPPING_COUNT  0     Number of ADaM variables mapped to multi… #> 5 TRACE_MEAN_TRACE_LEVEL         2.39  Mean trace level across all ADaM variabl…

title: End-to-End: From Evidence to Submission Decision
text: SCI sensitivity pillar weight variations
code: weight_grid <- data.frame(   quality   = c(0.35, 0.50, 0.25, 0.25),   trace     = c(0.25, 0.20, 0.40, 0.25),   risk      = c(0.25, 0.20, 0.25, 0.25),   usability = c(0.15, 0.10, 0.10, 0.25) ) scenario_labels <- c("Default", "Quality-heavy", "Trace-heavy", "Equal")  sens <- sci_sensitivity_analysis(evidence_pharma, weight_grid)  par(mar = c(4, 11, 3, 2)) barplot(   setNames(sens$SCI, scenario_labels),   horiz  = TRUE,   las    = 1,   col    = "#2C6DB5",   border = NA,   xlim   = c(0, 100),   xlab   = "SCI (0-100)",   main   = "SCI Sensitivity by Weight Scenario" ) abline(v = c(70, 85), lty = 2, col = "#888888")

title: End-to-End: From Evidence to Submission Decision
text: dashboard opens browser eight tabs: Overview · Evidence · Indicators · Pillars · Sensitivity · Risk · Traceability · Authority
code: library(r4subui) r4sub_app(evidence = evidence_pharma)

title: Authors and Citation
text: Pawan Rama Mali. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Mali P (2026). r4sub: Easily Install Load R4SUB Ecosystem. R package version 0.1.0, https://r4sub.github.io/r4sub/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {r4sub: Easily Install and Load the R4SUB Ecosystem},   author = {Pawan Rama Mali},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://r4sub.github.io/r4sub/}, }

title: r4sub — Ready for Submission
text: r4sub meta-package R4SUB (Ready Submission) clinical submission readiness ecosystem. single library(r4sub) call installs attaches core packages.

title: r4sub — Ready for Submission
text: See End--End vignette complete walkthrough: load evidence → score → profile → risk → traceability → dashboard.

title: r4sub — Ready for Submission
text: Development version:
code: install.packages("r4sub") pak::pak("R4SUB/r4sub")

title: r4sub — Ready for Submission
code: library(r4sub) # ── Attaching R4SUB packages ─────────────────────────────────────────── #   r4subcore      0.1.2 #   r4subtrace     0.1.1 #   r4subscore     0.1.1 #   r4subrisk      0.1.1 #   r4subdata      0.1.2 #   r4subprofile   0.1.1 #   r4subusability 0.1.0  # All ecosystem functions are now available data(evidence_pharma)                                    # r4subdata scores <- compute_indicator_scores(evidence_pharma)      # r4subscore sci    <- compute_sci(compute_pillar_scores(scores))     # r4subscore  # Ecosystem utilities r4sub_packages()   # versions and attachment status r4sub_status()     # installation check r4sub_conflicts()  # detect function name collisions r4sub_news()       # what changed in each package r4sub_cite()       # citation info for regulatory documents

title: r4sub — Ready for Submission
code: options(r4sub.quiet = TRUE) library(r4sub)

title: r4sub — Ready for Submission
text: Use :: operator explicit conflicts arise: Run r4sub_conflicts() library(r4sub) see collisions.
code: r4subcore::validate_evidence(ev) r4subscore::compute_sci(pillar_scores)

title: r4sub — Ready for Submission
text: MIT

title: Core R4SUB packages — core_packages
text: Returns names R4SUB packages automatically attached library(r4sub) called.

title: Core R4SUB packages — core_packages
code: core_packages()

title: Core R4SUB packages — core_packages
text: character vector package names.

title: Core R4SUB packages — core_packages
code: core_packages() #> [1] "r4subcore"    "r4subtrace"   "r4subscore"   "r4subrisk"    "r4subdata"    #> [6] "r4subprofile"

what: r4sub: Easily Install and Load the R4SUB Ecosystem — r4sub-package
title: r4sub: Easily Install and Load the R4SUB Ecosystem — r4sub-package
text: 'r4sub' package meta-package installs loads core packages R4SUB (R Regulatory Submission) clinical submission readiness ecosystem. Loading 'r4sub' attaches 'r4subcore', 'r4subtrace', 'r4subscore', 'r4subrisk', 'r4subdata', 'r4subprofile'. dashboard package 'r4subui' available auto-attached.

title: r4sub: Easily Install and Load the R4SUB Ecosystem — r4sub-package
text: Maintainer: Pawan Rama Mali prm@outlook.(ORCID) [copyright holder]

title: Cite the R4SUB Ecosystem — r4sub_cite
text: Prints citation information R4SUB ecosystem installed core package. Use citing R4SUB tools regulatory submission document, scientific paper, validation report.

title: Cite the R4SUB Ecosystem — r4sub_cite
code: r4sub_cite(package = NULL)

title: Cite the R4SUB Ecosystem — r4sub_cite
text: package Character. Name specific R4SUB package cite. NULL (default), cites installed core packages plus meta-package.

title: Cite the R4SUB Ecosystem — r4sub_cite
text: Invisibly returns named list citation objects, one per package. Called side effect printing citation information console.

title: Cite the R4SUB Ecosystem — r4sub_cite
code: r4sub_cite() #>  #> ── r4sub ── #>  #> To cite package ‘r4sub’ in publications use: #>  #>   Mali P (2026). _r4sub: Easily Install and Load the R4SUB Ecosystem_. #>   R package version 0.1.0, <https://r4sub.github.io/r4sub/>. #>  #> A BibTeX entry for LaTeX users is #>  #>   @Manual{, #>     title = {r4sub: Easily Install and Load the R4SUB Ecosystem}, #>     author = {Pawan Rama Mali}, #>     year = {2026}, #>     note = {R package version 0.1.0}, #>     url = {https://r4sub.github.io/r4sub/}, #>   } #> ── r4subcore ── #>  #> To cite package ‘r4subcore’ in publications use: #>  #>   Mali P (2026). _r4subcore: Core Data Contracts, Parsers, and Scoring #>   Primitives for Clinical Submission Readiness_. title: Show R4SUB Function Conflicts — r4sub_conflicts
text: Reports exported function name collisions attached R4SUB packages loaded packages.

title: Show R4SUB Function Conflicts — r4sub_conflicts
code: r4sub_conflicts()

title: Show R4SUB Function Conflicts — r4sub_conflicts
text: tibble columns: func, r4sub_package, other_package. Returned invisibly. Prints conflicts exist.

title: Show R4SUB Function Conflicts — r4sub_conflicts
code: r4sub_conflicts() #> ✔ No conflicts detected between R4SUB and other packages.

title: Show R4SUB Ecosystem News — r4sub_news
text: Prints NEWS entries installed R4SUB ecosystem package. Useful tracking changed across ecosystem updating.

title: Show R4SUB Ecosystem News — r4sub_news
code: r4sub_news(package = NULL)

title: Show R4SUB Ecosystem News — r4sub_news
text: package Character. Name specific R4SUB package show news . Must one core R4SUB packages. NULL (default), shows news installed core packages.

title: Show R4SUB Ecosystem News — r4sub_news
text: Invisibly returns named list news database objects, one per package. Called side effect printing NEWS entries console.

title: Show R4SUB Ecosystem News — r4sub_news
code: r4sub_news() #> ! No NEWS available for r4subcore. #>  #> ── r4subtrace ── #>  #>                         Changes in version 0.1.0                         #>  #>   - Initial CRAN release. #> ── r4subscore ── #>  #>                         Changes in version 0.1.0                         #>  #>   - Initial CRAN release. #> ── r4subrisk ── #>  #>                         Changes in version 0.1.0                         #>  #>   - Initial CRAN release. #> ── r4subdata ── #>  #>                         Changes in version 0.1.0                         #>  #>   - Initial CRAN release. #> ── r4subprofile ── #>  #>                         Changes in version 0.1.0                         #>  #>   - Initial CRAN release. r4sub_news("r4subcore") #> ! No NEWS available for r4subcore.

title: List R4SUB Ecosystem Packages — r4sub_packages
text: Returns tibble R4SUB packages installed version current attachment status.

title: List R4SUB Ecosystem Packages — r4sub_packages
code: r4sub_packages(include_self = TRUE)

title: List R4SUB Ecosystem Packages — r4sub_packages
text: include_self Logical. TRUE (default), includes r4sub meta-package listing.

title: List R4SUB Ecosystem Packages — r4sub_packages
text: tibble columns: package, version, attached.

title: List R4SUB Ecosystem Packages — r4sub_packages
code: r4sub_packages() #> # A tibble: 8 × 3 #>   package      version attached #>   <chr>        <chr>   <lgl>    #> 1 r4sub        0.1.0   TRUE     #> 2 r4subcore    0.1.0   TRUE     #> 3 r4subtrace   0.1.0   TRUE     #> 4 r4subscore   0.1.0   TRUE     #> 5 r4subrisk    0.1.0   TRUE     #> 6 r4subdata    0.1.1   TRUE     #> 7 r4subprofile 0.1.0   TRUE     #> 8 r4subui      0.1.0   FALSE

what: Check R4SUB Package Installation Status — r4sub_status
title: Check R4SUB Package Installation Status — r4sub_status
text: Reports installed version R4SUB ecosystem package highlights packages installed.

title: Check R4SUB Package Installation Status — r4sub_status
code: r4sub_status()

title: Check R4SUB Package Installation Status — r4sub_status
text: tibble columns: package, installed. Returned invisibly. Called side effect printing installation status console.

title: Check R4SUB Package Installation Status — r4sub_status
code: r4sub_status() #> ✔ 8 R4SUB packages installed.

title: r4sub 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-16 Initial CRAN release. core_packages(): list 6 auto-attached ecosystem packages. r4sub_packages(): show packages version attachment status. r4sub_status(): check ecosystem packages installed. r4sub_conflicts(): detect function name conflicts loaded packages. r4sub_news(): print NEWS ecosystem package. r4sub_cite(): print citation information regulatory academic use. Startup message can suppressed options(r4sub.quiet = TRUE).
